title: Battle of the Sacred Place
content:

|forces2=*Trade Federation outposts *[[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]s *[[P-series droideka/Legends|P-series destroyer droids]] *8 [[Plasma battle droid]]s *[[Trade Federation Patrol Boat]]s *An [[Ocean Troop Transport]] *One [[C-9979 landing craft/Legends|C-9979 landing craft]] *[[OOM command battle droid/Legends|OOM command battle droids]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=Light |casual2=Entire camp and forces in the surrounding area |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|Ha ha, deh mackineeks is destroy-ed, theysa fallen to Gungan army. Dissa muy good ting.
But mesa feelin' like mesa tinkin somethin' else comin' troo deh swamp. Maybe somethin' good dis time.|[[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Nass]]|}} A '''battle''' took place on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in the [[Gungan Sacred Place/Legends|Gungan Sacred Place]] following the evacuation of [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. ==The battle== ===Fleeing Otoh Gunga=== {{Quote|Uh-oh, Boss Daddy. Wesa got mackineeks settin' campen, not too far away.''"
"''Hmm...wesa better act soon. Best be wipen dem out quick.|A Gungan scout and Boss Nass|}} The battle took place on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] following the evacuation of [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. [[File:GB_Sacred_Place.png|thumb|left|200px|[[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Nass]] arrived at the [[Gungan Sacred Place/Legends|Gungan Sacred Place]].]] After escaping Otoh Gunga, [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]] and three [[Bongo Scout|Bongo Advanced Scouts]] battled several [[Trade Federation Patrol Boat]]s and [[Colo claw fish/Legends|colo claw fish]] before arriving at the [[Gungan Sacred Place/Legends|Gungan Sacred Place]] near the [[Gallo Mountains/Legends|Gallo Mountains]]. There, a large army of [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] troops who had likewise fled the city waited for him. As he arrived, a [[Scout/Legends|scout]] brought a report of a nearby Federation camp. [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]] commanded it and was nearby directing the landing of a [[C-9979 landing craft/Legends|droid transport ship]], but was not present at the camp itself. Nass ordered the destruction of the camp, and his Gungan troops carried out the task quickly. They destroyed all the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]s at the site and razed the buildings with few casualties. ===Arrival of the Naboo=== {{Quote|We do not wish to endanger your people. We shall traverse the swamp ourselves.|[[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala]], to Boss Nass|}} Boss Nass and the Gungans had no time to celebrate as [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] had just landed in the swamps along with [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], and their entourage. She asked for permission to journey to the Sacred Place. Boss Nass obliged them, and the Naboo group went through the swamps on foot. They battled many Trade Federation troops, who were sometimes already fighting members of the Resistance who had retreated into the swamps. At one point they were joined by two [[Scout (Royal Naboo)|Naboo light scout frigates]] and an [[Transport (Royal Naboo)|aquatic transport]], which they used to sink a Federation [[Ocean Troop Transport]] and to travel up the swamp. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Episode I Jedi Power Battles}} Along the way, the two Jedi[[Star Wars: Episode I Jedi Power Battles|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Jedi Power Battles'']] can be played with one or two characters; these characters are [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]], [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], and [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]]. Any character can be selected from the loading screen for any of the game's 10 missions. This article assumes the selection of Jinn and Kenobi, per their appearance in Episode I. fought through a large force of over 50 Federation battle droids in a dry riverbed, including one OOM-series command droid. The two Jedi also disrupted a command center. Moving past it, they destroyed 3 [[Plasma battle droid]]s and 26 more battle droids. After that, they encountered 5 more Plasma battle droids guarding a [[bridge]], along with more battle droids, some carrying [[Missile launcher/Legends|missile launchers]]. Past the bridge, the Jedi [[Force jump/Legends|Force Jumped]] through a set of ruins and avoided being eaten by [[Carnivorous plant (Naboo)|carnivorous plants]] and the [[kreetle]]s. After the ruins, they passed into the swamps and faced seven more battle droids and more carnivorous plants. They also avoided a Gungan pit trap full of kreetles. Near the sacred place, they were challenged by thirteen Gungan warriors; the two Jedi drove them off with non-lethal force. They then met the [[Gungan Security Chief]]. The Chief was bested in a short duel with the Jedi. During the duel, he leapt onto a statue several times to throw [[Booma/Legends|boomas]] or call in reinforcements—other Gungan warriors and kaadu. Thinking the Jedi had been worn down by his warriors, the Chief engaged the two Jedi again with his [[Electropole/Legends|electropole]], although he was defeated. Impressed, he let the Naboo proceed to see Boss Nass. {{Endgame}} ==Aftermath== {{Quote|Now dat Otoh Gunga issa no more, wesa must unite all deh Gungans at deh Sacred Place.|Boss Nass|}} They soon arrived at the Sacred Place, where Amidala revealed herself to Nass as the Queen of Naboo and begged for her help. Nass agreed to join with her to free Naboo. The Naboo security volunteers waited for Captain Panaka's orders,''[[Young Jedi Collectible Card Game]]'' as did several dozen members of the [[Naboo Underground]]. The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] secretly observed the proceedings using [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droids]].{{StoryCite|story=End Game|book=Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|bformatted=''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization, 2012 edition}} In large part because of Jar Jar Bink's role in the unification between the Gungans and the Naboo, Nass, in addition to revoking Bink's exile, also promoted the Gungan to [[Bombad General/Legends|Bombad General]], to Bink's horror. The Gungan composer [[Augara Jowil/Legends|Augara Jowil]] was present, and stunned by the events she had just witnessed, wrote the "[[Symponik Nabooalla]]" to commemorate the unification of the two peoples. The piece was later performed at the peace ceremony following the end of the war.{{DB|character|augie|Jowil, Augara "Augie"}} ==Behind the scenes== The Queen's meeting with the Gungans first appeared in materials relating to [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']],released in [[1999]]. Later releases filled in the details surrounding the meeting. Released in [[2000]] was the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Jedi Power Battles]]'' showed the Jedi as they approached the Sacred Place. The game's [[Star Wars: Episode I Jedi Power Battles: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] revealed ways through the mission. Defeating the battle droids is not the only option; many of them can be skipped. Beating the Gungan NPCs in the mission results in them running away, although it is possible to kill them permanently. Regardless, the player receives points for the action. The [[2001]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' showed the Gungans' retreat to the Sacred Place. [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|The game's strategy guide]] detailed the best way to pass the mission. The guide recommended leading Nass and company slowly toward the sacred place to avoid one of the creatures. After the Federation outpost was destroyed, the game switches perspective and controls to that of the Queen and her escorts. Two Naboo repeater troopers replace the pilots and guards seen in the movie. The guide recommends sending Jinn and Kenobi ahead to deal with ambushes, then retreating to heal their hitpoints. The game ends if any of the named "hero" characters die. Using a cheat code to show the entirety of the map shows OOM-9 in the corner with a landing craft. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' *[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth
